div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
THE SCALING LAW — and WHY it saturates
(the 'sticker collector' rule)

div.style: color: rgb(58, 42, 102) !important; display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;

span.p:
There are only 8 popular routes
everyone tends to ask about.
(think: 8 stickers to collect)

div.style: color: rgb(51, 66, 79) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
The FIRST worker is the one who
fills the whiteboard in: it pays to look
up each of the 8 routes once.

div.style: color: rgb(28, 82, 48) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Every worker AFTER the first
finds all 8 already on the board.
=> each extra worker saves ~8 trips.

div.style: color: rgb(27, 58, 102) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
So along the AGENTS dial:
savings grow in a STRAIGHT LINE.
uplift is about 8 x (number of extra workers)

div.style: color: rgb(94, 61, 18) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Along the TURNS dial it FLATTENS OUT:
once the board has all 8 routes,
talking longer finds nothing new to cache.
(you already collected every sticker)

div.style: color: rgb(107, 84, 16) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
The whole surface in one line:
uplift = (agents - 1) x pool x (1 - (1 - 1/pool) ^ (shared-reads))
'every extra worker gets every route the first one already collected'

div.style: color: rgb(28, 82, 48) !important; display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
This formula was DERIVED first,
then matched the kernel's real numbers
with ZERO knobs to tune. The data agrees.
